mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the preceding 3 months signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.37% on 2016-04-01 indicates accommodative policy stance
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% on 2016-04-01 indicates near-full employment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that portends lower corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to activate.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 453438M on 2016-04-01, HOUST at 1089K on 2016-03-01
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or the joint signal produces positive returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over 2 months indicates supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization, signaling lower profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.3759 on 2016-03-01 indicates moderate industrial activity
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward returns during INDPRO decline periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both indicators to move favorably simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 150K month-over-month AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 143915K on 2016-04-01, UNRATE at 5.0% on 2016-04-01
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or positive 20-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances despite favorable labor signal.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, monetary policy enters a restrictive regime that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.37% on 2016-04-01 is well below restrictive threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter at an annualized rate above 2.5% signals sustained economic expansion that supports equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism operates as an always-on trend signal.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16492.7B (2017 dollars) on 2016-01-01 indicates positive growth trajectory
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward returns during periods of GDPC1 growth > 2.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint signal combining rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 1.5%) indicates monetary policy is tightening in response to price pressures, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as the market reprices real earnings and discount rates simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.92 on 2016-03-01, FEDFUNDS at 0.37% on 2016-04-01
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or positive 20-day returns occur in > 50% of triggered instances despite inflation-tightening signal.
